source_jobs.title: Senior Mulesoft Developer
source_jobs.description:
Senior MuleSoft Developer



About the role

We’re looking for a Senior MuleSoft Developer to help design, build, and scale enterprise integrations that connect critical business systems across the company. This is a senior individual contributor role for someone who can lead solution design, influence architecture, and partner closely with both technical and non-technical stakeholders to deliver scalable, durable integrations.

What you’ll do

 - Design and implement end-to-end integration solutions using MuleSoft and related APIs across enterprise platforms.

 - Provide technical guidance to developers and help drive alignment with architectural standards, best practices, and long-term scalability.

 - Partner with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into sustainable technical designs.

 - Lead solution reviews, troubleshoot complex integration issues, and improve reliability across connected systems.

 - Coach less senior engineers and raise the bar on engineering quality, maintainability, and delivery.

What we’re looking for

 - 8+ years of experience building integrations in large-scale enterprise environments, with deep hands-on expertise in MuleSoft(including Data Weave)

 - Strong experience with REST APIs, SOAP services, event-driven integrations, security, and enterprise integration patterns.

 - Proven ability to design and deliver complex technical solutions with measurable business impact.

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to present technical concepts clearly to a wide range of stakeholders.

 - Experience mentoring engineers and influencing technical direction without direct authority.

Nice to have

 - Experience integrating with or building on Salesforce is a strong plus.

 - Background with Kafka, Five9 (or other Telephony Integrations), CI/CD, or enterprise platform modernization.

 - Experience as a Technical Lead or Solution Architect.

source_jobs.title: Sr Salesforce Developer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role:

At Riveron, a nationally recognized business advisory firm, we blend deep industry expertise with innovative technology to deliver impactful solutions. We’re looking for a Salesforce Developer to join our growing Technology & Innovation team. In this role, you’ll help build secure, scalable Salesforce solutions that empower core business processes across the enterprise.



Who You Are:

• You have 5+ years of Salesforce development experience, with strong expertise in Apex, Lightning Web Components (LWC), Visualforce, and SOQL.

• You’ve built robust integrations using REST/SOAP APIs, platform events, and tools like MuleSoft.

• You understand Salesforce architecture, data models, and security best practices.

• You’re skilled with declarative tools such as Flows, Validation Rules, Process Builder, and Approval Processes.

• You thrive in Agile environments, actively participating in sprints, code reviews, and technical discussions.

• You are familiar with CI/CD processes and tools like Git, Copado, Gearset, and have experience using Salesforce DX.

• You hold a Salesforce Platform Developer I certification (required), and other certifications like Platform Developer II, Admin, or App Builder are a strong plus.

• You’re a collaborative team player and mentor who can bridge the gap between technical and business needs.

• You write clean, scalable code and follow Salesforce best practices to ensure long-term maintainability.



What You’ll Do:

• Develop, customize, and maintain applications on the Salesforce platform.

• Create and optimize custom objects, Apex classes, triggers, and Lightning components (LWC).

• Design and implement secure, scalable integrations with external platforms.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and translate them into technical solutions.

• Automate business processes using declarative tools and custom development as needed.

• Participate in sprint planning, technical design reviews, and deployment cycles.

• Troubleshoot and resolve system issues, ensuring minimal disruption to end-users.

• Support DevOps initiatives including code versioning, automated testing, and deployment pipelines.

• Contribute to documentation, process improvement, and knowledge sharing across the development team.

source_jobs.title: Restructuring and Turnaround Services - Associate Director
source_jobs.description:
When faced with unexpected challenges or periods of distress, companies need to make fast, informed decisions and implement straightforward solutions to get back on track. Our experienced professionals quickly stabilize the business, diagnose the issues, and create a strategic plan that will drive the best possible outcomes for the company and stakeholders.

Whether seeking to preserve capital, improve cash flow, or restore earnings, we draw on our proven solutions, industry depth, and operational expertise to implement the change our clients need.

Our Restructuring and Turnaround services include Performance Improvement, Restructuring & Turnaround Advisory, Interim Management, and Bankruptcy & Fiduciary. Industries we serve include Aerospace, Defense, & Government Contractors, Automotive & Industrials, Business Services, Consumer Products & Retail, Energy, Engineering & Construction, Financial Services, Government, Municipal, & Education, Healthcare, Hospitality & Entertainment, Real Estate, Technology, Media & Telecommunications.



Who You Are:

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field of study

 - Minimum 7 years of finance, accounting, or related experience

 - Extensive experience with Microsoft Excel, including conditional formatting, lookup formulas, pivot tables, protecting files, creating scenario and what-if analyses, modifying options, and applying data validation to worksheets

 - Experience related to direct cash flow forecasting that integrates with income statement and balance sheet, running sensitivity analysis, advanced financial analysis, and account reconciliation

 - Experience with automating processes, improving controls, valuation analysis of distressed companies using EBITDA multiples and discounted cash flow

 - Experience related to distressed companies’ financial analysis/recommendation, bankruptcy/reorganization, and strategic growth consulting and analysis

 - You have a passion for developing and maintaining client relationships

 - You get the job done and have fun doing it

 - You have experience working with large, imperfect data sets

 - You readily identify problems and instinctively look for solutions

 - You demonstrate analytical rigor and strong written and verbal communication skills

 - You thrive in an ever-changing, dynamic work environment

 - You enjoy participating in internal and external company initiatives such as community service, training, recruiting, and firm events

 - You have the ability and desire to travel as required based on client location



What You'll Do:

 - Use financial and/or operational acumen to provide solutions to interested parties in situations related to underperforming, distressed, or bankrupt businesses

 - Oversee research, data and financial analysis, and development of financial models, reports, presentations, and other client-facing deliverables

 - Assist in developing engagement strategy and scoping client deliverables

 - Establish expectations for project team members, lead select client communication, own project workstreams, and ensure deadlines are met

 - Participate actively in the development of junior team members by providing coaching and performance feedback, and fostering a team-based working culture
   
   

Applications for this evergreen opportunity will be accepted through Dec 31, 2026.

source_jobs.title: Tax Advisory - M&A Director
source_jobs.description:
As an M&A Director in our Tax Advisory practice, you will partner directly with our clients to help them navigate the dynamic tax challenges associated with mergers and acquisitions. You’ll use your technical expertise and real-time command of tax policy to advise clients across industries on strategic issues such as risk mitigation, M&A tax implications, and tax function optimization. 



In addition to leading a team of consultants and owning vital client relationships, at the Director level you will enhance your business operations toolkit by supporting executive leadership’s strategy for developing the practice and increasing Riveron’s brand recognition in the market. Our culture of continuous learning means that here, there are always opportunities to accelerate your growth. 



Who You Are:

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in Accounting or Taxation

 - Minimum 10 years of experience in M&A tax advisory or consulting

 - You have a passion for developing client relationships and an appetite for building new skills

 - You are skilled at leading and developing teams and individuals

 - You thrive in an ever-changing, dynamic work environment

 - You are a self-starter who readily identifies problems and instinctively looks for solutions

 - You believe that exceeding client expectations and ~having fun~ at work are mutually dependent

 - You enjoy participating in internal and external company initiatives such as community service, training, recruiting, committees, ERGs, conference panels, and firm events 

 - You have the ability and desire to travel as required based on client location



What You’ll Do:

 - Use tax technical knowledge to perform due diligence and advise clients on complex M&A tax implications, liabilities, and strategies

 - Conduct analysis to assess federal and state tax implications associated with transactions, and execute tax integration of acquired entities

 - Advise clients on structuring acquisitions, design and prepare acquisition transaction steps and analysis, and incorporate tax structuring advice from other jurisdictions

 - Contribute actively to the development of people leaders into future practice leaders

 - Provide guidance and issue resolution support to teams across multiple concurrent client engagements

 - Identify and secure opportunities for revenue growth and service expansion with existing clients

source_jobs.title: Sr. Associate - Integration Engineer (Celigo)
source_jobs.description:
Job Summary

We are seeking an experienced Senior Integration Developer specializing in Celigo to design, develop, and manage scalable integrations across enterprise systems. The role requires strong expertise in integration architecture, API development, and workflow automation using the Celigo platform. The ideal candidate will drive end-to-end integration solutions, ensure system reliability, and support business process optimization.

Key Responsibilities

 - Design, develop, test, and deploy integrations using Celigo (Integrator.io http://Integrator.io platform).

 - Architect scalable and reusable integration solutions across ERP, CRM, eCommerce, and third-party systems.

 - Lead complex integration projects, including requirements gathering, solution design, and implementation.

 - Build and maintain REST/SOAP API integrations and manage data transformations.

 - Develop and optimize Celigo flows, scripts, and error-handling mechanisms.

 - Troubleshoot and resolve integration issues, ensuring minimal downtime and high performance.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams (business, IT, external vendors) to align integration solutions with business needs.

 - Ensure data accuracy, security, and compliance across all integrations.

 - Maintain documentation for integration architecture, workflows, and processes.

 - Mentor junior developers and provide technical guidance on best practices.

Required Skills & Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience in integration development, with 4+ years of hands-on experience in Celigo (Integrator.io http://Integrator.io).

 - Strong understanding of API-based integrations (REST, SOAP, JSON, XML).

 - Experience integrating systems such as NetSuite, Salesforce, Shopify, or other enterprise applications.

 - Proficiency in JavaScript for scripting within Celigo or similar platforms.

 - Solid knowledge of data mapping, transformation, and ETL concepts.

 - Experience with error handling, monitoring, and performance tuning of integrations.

 - Strong problem-solving and debugging skills.

 - Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with NetSuite integrations and SuiteScript is highly desirable.

 - Exposure to other iPaaS platforms (Workato, MuleSoft, Boomi) is a plus.

 - Knowledge of Alteryx or other data engineering tools for data preparation and workflow automation.

 - Familiarity with Salesforce, NetSuite, other CRM & ERP tools and integration patterns.

source_jobs.title: Transaction Services - Data Analytics Senior Associate
source_jobs.description:
As a Transaction Services Data Analytics Senior Associate at Riveron, you will provide crucial data-driven insights for complex transactions involving private equity and corporate clients. Your role analyzes extensive datasets to uncover trends and deliver actionable recommendations that guide clients through due diligence, post-deal performance, and exit strategies. You’ll translate complex data into clear insights for clients and internal teams, ensuring strategic decisions are well-informed. In this dynamic, fast-paced environment, you will stay ahead of industry trends and emerging technologies, enhancing our analytical approach, and delivering impactful results throughout the deal lifecycle. 

Who You Are:

 - Bachelor’s or master's in accounting, data science, data analytics, statistics, economics, or related field 

 - CPA and/or audit experience preferred but not required 

 - Minimum 2 years of total relevant experience involving a combination of financial due diligence for transaction services and data analytics 

 - Minimum 1 years of transaction experience focused on quality of earnings, net working capital, and cash flows 

 - Minimum 1years of experience with one or a combination of the following: 

 - Data Visualization Tools: Experience with D3.js http://D3.js, Tableau, PowerBI, Spotfire, or similar platforms. 

 - Data Management: Proficiency in Hadoop, SQL, Alteryx, or comparable technologies. 

 - Analytics Platforms: Knowledge of SAS, Azure, or related systems. 

 - Statistical Software: Familiarity with SPSS, Minitab, or similar packages. 

 - Programming Languages: Competence in Python, R, or other relevant languages. 

 - You thrive in an ever-changing, dynamic work environment 

 - You readily identify problems and instinctively look for solutions 

 - You demonstrate analytical rigor and strong written and verbal communication skills  

 - You have the ability and desire to travel as required based on client location 



What You'll Do:

 - Analyze extensive datasets using data management technologies (e.g., Alteryx, Hadoop, SQL) to develop cleansed and organized datasets that will serve as input into M&A models, adjustments to EBITDA tables, net working capital analyses, or other key financial metrics. 

 - Employ statistical software (e.g., SPSS, Minitab) and programming languages (e.g., Python, R) to perform in-depth analysis and identify trends, risks, and opportunities within transaction data. 

 - Translate complex data findings into clear, strategic recommendations for due diligence and transaction models. 

 - Utilize the application of analytics platforms (e.g., SAS, Azure) throughout the transaction lifecycle to make high-quality, data-driven decision-making and insights. 

 - Stay updated with industry trends and emerging technologies to enhance our data analytics capabilities and support the delivery of impactful results for clients. 

Applications for this evergreen opportunity will be accepted through Dec 31, 2026.

source_jobs.title: Associate - Business Process Risk Advisory
source_jobs.description:
In today’s dynamic environment, business leaders face constantly shifting risks. Riveron helps organizations implement leading governance, risk and compliance practices by combining deep expertise with pragmatic partnership, using a hands-on approach to understand the specific needs of the organization and create tailored solutions to address key compliance risks.

Our Risk Advisory services include SOX readiness, managed internal controls testing and monitoring, co-sourced/outsourced internal audit, segregation of duties and access risk review, policy and procedure development, enterprise risk management, and IT and cybersecurity risk assessment.

The Senior Associate level position for Riveron’s Risk Advisory group will work collaboratively with senior team members who provide guidance, coaching, and direction. The role includes assessing the design and operating effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting (ICFR), developing and executing remediation roadmaps, and performing ICFR risk assessments.



Qualifications:

 - Bachelor's and/or Master’s degree in Accounting

 - Relevant certification preferred, CPA, CIA and/or CISA

 - Minimum 1 year experience, the majority of which is within external audit or risk advisory roles

 - Demonstrated knowledge of business risks, internal controls over financial reporting, SOX, the COSO framework
   
   

Who You Are:

 - You have a passion for developing and maintaining client relationships

 - You get the job done and have fun doing it

 - You communicate skillfully with a variety of audiences and can create compelling stories from data

 - You thrive in an ever-changing, dynamic work environment

 - You readily identify problems and instinctively look for solutions

source_jobs.title: Tax Manager
source_jobs.description:
Riveron collaborates with companies to navigate the constantly shifting tax regulatory landscape, offering comprehensive solutions to mitigate, defend, or optimize tax positions. source_jobs.title: Salesforce Technical Architect
source_jobs.description:
Salesforce Technical Architect –

Role Overview:

Salesforce Technical Architect is responsible for designing and implementing scalable, secure, and high-performing Salesforce solutions across multiple clouds .This role provides technical leadership, ensures adherence to best practices, and collaborates with stakeholders to deliver enterprise-grade solutions.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Define end-to-end Salesforce architecture for complex business processes.

 - Ensure solutions align with business requirements and Salesforce best practices.

 - Create detailed architecture diagrams, integration patterns, and data strategies.

 - Design integration strategy with external systems using REST/SOAP APIs and middleware such as MuleSoft and Boomi.

 - Own technical decisions around Apex, LWC, APIs, and middleware integration.

 - Establish data governance, migration strategies, and performance optimization.

 - Guide development teams on Apex, Lightning Web Components (LWC), and declarative tools.

 - Conduct code reviews and enforce security and scalability standards.

 - Work closely with business analysts, product owners, and solution architects.

 - Ensure solutions comply with Salesforce governor limits and security standards.

 - Optimize system performance for large data volumes.

 - Define and manage data migration strategies, mappings, and loading sequences.

 - Set and enforce coding standards, modular design patterns, Code reusability and platform limits compliance.

 - Lead DevOps and CI/CD pipeline strategies (Git, Azure DevOps, Copado, Gearset).

 - Review and optimize performance, scalability, and reliability of the system.

 - Review and approve Apex classes, triggers, batches, LWC components, and integration logic.

 - Support system testing, performance testing, and UAT cycles.

 - Maintain architecture documentation and technical design documents.



Required Skills & Qualifications:

 - 10 to 15 years of experience in Salesforce with at least 5 years as technical architect.

 - Deep knowledge of Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud, and AppExchange solutions.

 - Hands-on experience with Apex, LWC and Integrations

 - Experience with CI/CD, DevOps tools, and version control.

 - Familiarity with integration tools such as MuleSoft, Dell Boomi etc.

 - 10 to 15 Years of Experience in Salesforce with at least 5 years as Solution Architect

 - Strong knowledge of Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud and Revenue Cloud

 - Strong Knowledge of Apex and LWC.

 - Experience with integration middleware’s such as MuleSoft, Boomi etc.

 - Strong knowledge of Agent force and custom actions

 - Knowledge of CI/CD, DevOps practices, and version control

 - Experience with one of DevOps platforms such as Copado/Gearset

 - Knowledge of AppExchange products such as DocuSign, Conga, Demand tools, Cloudingo, Checkmarks, PMD, OwnBackup, Vonage

 - Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.



Certifications:

 - Salesforce Certified Application Architect

 - Platform Developer I & II

 - Integration Architecture Designer

 - Salesforce Certified Technical Architect



Nice-to-Have:

 - Knowledge of CPQ and Revenue Cloud

source_jobs.title: Sr Associate - Salesforce QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Role Overview

The Salesforce QA Engineer is responsible for ensuring the quality and reliability of Salesforce applications and integrations. The ideal candidate will have strong experience in testing Salesforce solutions, with a particular focus on Salesforce CPQ functionality, expertise in QA automation, and a solid understanding of Salesforce Sales Cloud. Exposure to Salesforce Billing is preferred, while experience with Service Cloud and Experience Cloud is desirable.

Key Responsibilities

 - Prepare and execute test strategies, test plans, test cases, and test scripts for Salesforce applications, with a primary focus on Salesforce CPQ and Sales Cloud.

 - Design, execute, and maintain functional, regression, integration, sanity, and user acceptance testing (UAT) for Salesforce implementations.

 - Define test environment and test data requirements, and collaborate with development teams to ensure test readiness.

 - Validate Salesforce CPQ business processes, including product configuration, pricing and product rules, quoting, approvals, amendments, renewals, and order management.

 - Perform integration testing to ensure data accuracy and integrity across Salesforce objects and integrated systems.

 - Prepare, automate, and execute regression test suites to maximize test coverage and improve release quality.

 - Collaborate with developers, business analysts, product owners, and business stakeholders to clarify requirements and identify defects early in the development lifecycle.

 - Conduct sanity testing to ensure applications are production-ready before deployment.

 - Track, prioritize, and report defects using Jira or similar tools, ensuring timely resolution.

 - Participate in Agile ceremonies and contribute to continuous improvement of QA processes and automation practices.

Required Skills & Qualifications

 - Minimum 5 years of experience in functional and automation testing of Salesforce applications.

 - At least 2 years of hands-on experience in Salesforce CPQ functionality testing.

 - Strong understanding of Salesforce Sales Cloud business processes and functionality.

 - Expertise in at least one QA automation tool, such as Provar, Selenium, or an equivalent automation framework.

 - Experience in preparing test strategies, test plans, test scenarios, and detailed test cases.

 - Hands-on experience with API testing (REST/SOAP) and integration validation.

 - Strong understanding of the Salesforce data model, security model, and platform customizations.

 - Experience with regression test automation and test execution.

 - Proficiency in Agile/Scrum methodologies.

 - Hands-on experience using Jira or similar defect management tools.

 - Experience working in client-facing roles with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

Preferred Skills

 - Exposure to Salesforce Billing functionality and testing.

 - Experience conducting load and performance testing.

 - Knowledge of Revenue Cloud processes.

 - Experience validating Salesforce integrations with external applications.

Desirable Skills

 - Exposure to Salesforce Service Cloud.

 - Exposure to Salesforce Experience Cloud.

 - Knowledge of Salesforce automation frameworks such as TestNG.

Certifications (Preferred)

 - Provar Automation Certification

 - Selenium WebDriver Certification

 - You communicate skillfully with a variety of audiences and can create compelling stories from data

 - You thrive in an ever-changing, dynamic work environment 

 - You readily identify problems and instinctively look for solutions 

 - You enjoy participating in internal and external company initiatives such as community service, training, recruiting, and firm events

source_jobs.title: Technology and Innovation - Data Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
Data Engineering Manager

Riveron is seeking a hands-on Data Engineering Manager to be part of our data engineering team in India. This is not a pure people-management role — you will be writing code, building pipelines, and architecting data platforms daily alongside your team. We're looking for someone who leads from the codebase, not just from a conference room. If you bring deep implementation experience across Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and Databricks and can pair strong architectural thinking with real delivery output, we'd love to hear from you.

Who You Are:

 - A data engineering professional with 7-9 years of experience, including 1-3 years balancing technical leadership with active, hands-on implementation — you've managed teams without stepping away from the keyboard.

 - You write production-quality code daily. This role requires active coding and pipeline development — not just code reviews and architectural diagrams.

 - Hands-on, implementation-level expertise with Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and Databricks is non-negotiable. You've personally built, deployed, and tuned production workloads on these platforms.

 - Strong data architecture background spanning lakehouse design, medallion architecture, data vault, dimensional modeling, and enterprise data platform design — grounded in systems you've actually built, not just designed on paper.

 - Deep proficiency in SQL and Python — you can write complex transformations, optimize query performance, debug pipeline failures, and build automation frameworks yourself.

 - Proven experience building and shipping ETL/ELT frameworks using Azure Data Factory, dbt, Spark, and platform-native orchestration tools.

 - Solid working knowledge of Azure cloud data services (Synapse Analytics, Data Lake Storage, Azure SQL Database) and comfort operating in multi-cloud environments.

 - Well-versed in data governance, data quality frameworks, cataloging, lineage, and security best practices — with experience implementing these controls, not just defining policies.

 - Proficient with Git-based version control, CI/CD pipelines for data workloads, and Infrastructure as Code — you commit code, open pull requests, and maintain deployment pipelines alongside your team.

 - Comfortable working in Agile delivery environments and translating business requirements into scalable technical designs.

What You'll Do:

 - Spend a significant portion of your time writing code — building data pipelines, developing transformation logic, configuring platform services, and shipping production-ready solutions.

 - Lead, mentor, and grow a team of data engineers while remaining the team's strongest technical contributor and setting the standard through your own code and architectural decisions.

 - Design and personally implement scalable data platforms on Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and Databricks — selecting the right platform for each use case and building the solution end to end.

 - Architect and build lakehouse and data warehouse solutions, defining and coding ingestion patterns, transformation layers, storage strategies, and consumption models.

 - Own critical and complex implementation work — the toughest pipeline builds, the most challenging performance problems, and the architectural spikes that set direction for the team.

 - Write and maintain shared libraries, frameworks, and reusable components that accelerate the team's delivery and enforce engineering standards.

 - Conduct deep, line-level code reviews focused on correctness, performance, and maintainability — not just approval stamps.

 - Partner with analytics, data science, and business stakeholders to translate data requirements into engineered solutions, and personally prototype key components.

 - Establish and enforce best practices for data modeling, pipeline reliability, observability, testing, and documentation — by building the reference implementations yourself.

 - Optimize platform performance and manage costs acro

 - Monitor server performance and ensure infrastructure availability.

Microsoft Intune & Device Management

 - Troubleshoot Microsoft 365, Teams, Outlook, VPN, and endpoint-related issues.

 - Resolve user access, profile, and application-related incidents.

Security & Compliance

 - Monitor privileged access and investigate security incidents.

Automation & Documentation

 - Automate administrative tasks using PowerShell scripting.

 - Generate user, access, and compliance reports.

 - Provide technical guidance and support to internal teams.

At Riveron, a nationally recognized business advisory firm, we blend deep industry expertise with innovative technology to deliver impactful solutions.
